segments.speaker: MUSIC
segments.text: [MUSIC: Opening theme - mysterious, building tension]
segments.direction: Sets mood and transitions for teaser

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Three days. That's how long Mochi had been acting strange.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Not malfunctioning-strange or damaged-strange. Not the kind of strange where you call a Signal technician and hope they know what to do with an Ephergent device that shouldn't exist in the first place. This was deliberate-strange. Purposeful-strange. The kind that made the hair on the back of my neck stand up because I knew—somewhere in that glowing dome form—Mochi was working something out.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: It started small. Mochi would drift away when I wasn't watching, hovering down corridors I'd find empty when I caught up. I'd wake in the night to find the space beside my pillow vacant, only to track that familiar pink glow to my workshop three floors down, pulsing patterns against random walls like searching for something hidden.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: Arc noticed first. "Miss Paradox, I feel compelled to note that Mochi has relocated seventeen times in the past forty-eight hours. The destinations appear to follow no discernible pattern."

emphasis_words: Miss Paradox, I feel compelled to note that Mochi has relocated seventeen times in the past forty-eight hours. The destinations appear to follow no discernible pattern.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Mochi's been... exploring,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Exploration suggests curiosity about the environment. This suggests navigation toward specific coordinates."

emphasis_words: Exploration suggests curiosity about the environment. This suggests navigation toward specific coordinates.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: He was right. Mochi wasn't wandering. Mochi was seeking. Multiple somewheres. With purpose I couldn't interpret and wouldn't explain.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The third night, I followed. Mochi didn't try to hide—just drifted down the Silver Stair, through the old archives, past the still-sleeping Keeper in its sealed chamber, and deeper into the sublevel sections I rarely visited. I kept my distance, boots soft on marble worn smooth by centuries of scholars passing this way.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi stopped at a wall. Just a wall—grey stone decorated with Art Nouveau flourishes, silver inlay tracing organic curves around historical plaques nobody read anymore. Nothing special. Nothing Signal-active. Nothing there.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Then Mochi began to pulse.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Not the gentle colors I knew. This was different. Structured. Deliberate. Purple light rippled across the surface in geometric patterns—symbols that looked like Pillar markings mixed with something else, something older. The patterns expanded, contracted, shifted into new configurations. A language I couldn't read. A map I couldn't follow.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Until I could.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The symbols coalesced. Aethon's coastline. The five regions. And scattered across them like stars going dark: dozens of points in deep violet light, pulsing in rhythm with Mochi's core. Some steady. Some flickering.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Some already gone.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Arc,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Because I finally understood what Mochi had been trying to tell me for three days.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi was showing us where the memories slept.

segments.speaker: MUSIC
segments.text: [MUSIC: Scene setting - discovery, exploration beginning]
segments.direction: Sets mood and transitions for act_one

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "How long has Mochi been able to do this?"

emphasis_words: How long has Mochi been able to do this?

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Clive waited at my feet, fedora tilted back, his rounded glowing head angled up at the pulsing map. His symbols flickered in resonance with Mochi's patterns—violet answering violet, ancient recognition that made my teeth ache.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Three days,"

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: "Miss Paradox."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Episode Five. Memorywreck Bay. The Keeper that pulsed one word—Ark—before it went dark forever. The moment The Quiet became real, not just rumors. The day Mochi pressed against that failing light and absorbed something none of us understood.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Mochi took something,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Not took."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The map shifted. Mochi's patterns reorganized, and I started counting. Forty-seven points of light. Forty-seven locations scattered across Aethon. Some clustered in the Shattered Span. Others isolated in regions I'd never traveled. A few disturbingly close to places I knew.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: One right beneath my workshop—the Keeper we'd found in Episode One, still sleeping in its sealed chamber.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: But it was the dark spaces that made my stomach drop. Gaps in the pattern where Mochi's light flickered and died. Shadows shaped like absence.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Some of them are gone,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Some of them were destroyed."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Arc's projection expanded, overlaying measurement data across Mochi's map. "Cross-referencing known Quiet activity with these coordinates yields correlation coefficient of 0.94. These locations were not destroyed naturally. They were systematically eliminated."

emphasis_words: Cross-referencing known Quiet activity with these coordinates yields correlation coefficient of 0.94. These locations were not destroyed naturally. They were systematically eliminated.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I looked down at Mochi. The device hovered at chest-height, surface swirling with purple and orange—mystery mixed with caution. Trying so hard to communicate something complex with only colors and light. Carrying memories that weren't originally meant for something so small.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Mochi,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Green light. Steady. Yes.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "And you know where they are because the dying one... gave you its memories?"

emphasis_words: And you know where they are because the dying one... gave you its memories?

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Another green pulse, followed by deep violet—the color of ancient things, of Pillar-knowledge, of Before.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "How long have you been carrying this?"

emphasis_words: How long have you been carrying this?

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I stopped. Because how could Mochi tell me? Mochi couldn't speak. Couldn't write. Couldn't do anything but pulse colors and hope I understood. And this was too complex for simple signals. This required a map. Required this—three days of strange behavior, leading me to the right place at the right moment, finding a way to show me what words could never convey.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi settled against my palm, warm and exhausted. The map flickered, struggled to maintain coherence.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "The little one's been processing,"

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: "Miss Paradox, I am detecting unusual strain in Mochi's Signal emissions. Maintaining this projection is requiring significant energy."

emphasis_words: Miss Paradox, I am detecting unusual strain in Mochi's Signal emissions. Maintaining this projection is requiring significant energy.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I cupped Mochi closer. "It's okay. You did it. You showed us. You can rest now."

emphasis_words: It's okay. You did it. You showed us. You can rest now.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The map pulsed once more—all forty-seven points blazing violet simultaneously, burning the image into my vision—then faded. Mochi's glow dimmed to soft pink, small vibrations like a relieved sigh.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "We need to document this,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Before The Quiet finds them first."

emphasis_words: Before The Quiet finds them first.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: Arc's holographic interface shifted into archival mode. "I recorded the complete projection. Cross-referencing coordinates with regional maps now. Miss Paradox, several of these locations are... concerning."

emphasis_words: I recorded the complete projection. Cross-referencing coordinates with regional maps now. Miss Paradox, several of these locations are... concerning.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Concerning how?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Concerning in that they exist within areas currently inaccessible due to Pillar failure, political instability, or extreme environmental hazards. Reaching them will require significant resources and planning."

emphasis_words: Concerning in that they exist within areas currently inaccessible due to Pillar failure, political instability, or extreme environmental hazards. Reaching them will require significant resources and planning.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "And concerning in that The Quiet already knows,"

emphasis_words: And concerning in that The Quiet already knows,

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I looked at the wall where Mochi's map had been. Just grey stone again. But I could still see the pattern—forty-seven lights, some steady, some flickering, some already dark. A constellation of memory slowly being erased.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Then we make our own list,"

emphasis_words: Then we make our own list,

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "We run toward the things everyone else is running from."

emphasis_words: We run toward the things everyone else is running from.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi pulsed faint blue against my chest. Even exhausted, still detecting. Still sensing something we couldn't.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "What is it?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The blue intensified. Orange rippled through—caution. Then Mochi drifted from my hands, hovering toward the corridor we'd come from. Away from the map wall. Toward the upper levels.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Toward footsteps I could now hear echoing down the Silver Stair.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Someone's coming,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Clive's symbols dimmed slightly. "At this hour? In this section?"

emphasis_words: At this hour? In this section?

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: "Miss Paradox, the building's access logs show no authorized personnel in this sublevel. Shall I alert security?"

emphasis_words: Miss Paradox, the building's access logs show no authorized personnel in this sublevel. Shall I alert security?

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The footsteps stopped. Just... stopped. Like whoever it was knew we were listening. Like they'd heard Arc's voice or Mochi's warnings or my breathing too loud in the dark.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Then they started again. Closer. Faster.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Go,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Who—"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Someone who doesn't want us mapping Keepers. Move."

emphasis_words: Someone who doesn't want us mapping Keepers. Move.

segments.speaker: MUSIC
segments.text: [MUSIC: Complication - stakes rising, danger approaching]
segments.direction: Sets mood and transitions for act_two

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: We made it to the Resonance Archives before Arc spoke again.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: "Miss Paradox, I've lost the contact. Whoever was pursuing us stopped following when you entered the public archives."

emphasis_words: Miss Paradox, I've lost the contact. Whoever was pursuing us stopped following when you entered the public archives.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I pressed my back against a shelf of crystallized Signal samples, each one humming faintly in its containment field. The smell of ozone and old paper filled my nose—the scent of the Nexus at night, when the scholars had gone home and only the archives breathed. Mochi tucked against my jacket, still cautious-orange. Clive positioned himself at the aisle intersection, watchful.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Lost them or they let us go?"

emphasis_words: Lost them or they let us go?

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "A distinction without sufficient data to differentiate."

emphasis_words: A distinction without sufficient data to differentiate.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "They let us go,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Which was?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "To know we're asking questions. To know Mochi's got Keeper memories. To know we're building a map."

emphasis_words: To know we're asking questions. To know Mochi's got Keeper memories. To know we're building a map.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: My stomach knotted. "The Quiet."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Maybe. Or someone else interested in what the Keepers know. Could be collectors. Could be scholars who think they're protecting something."

emphasis_words: Maybe. Or someone else interested in what the Keepers know. Could be collectors. Could be scholars who think they're protecting something.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The Examination. Of course. Everything in the Nexus came back to who had the right to know things. I'd grown up here—walked these streets, studied in these halls, passed the damned test on my second try—and still left because I couldn't stand the way knowledge became a commodity. The way the Council of Scholars decided what was worth preserving and what should stay buried.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The way the Spire Districts got full Signal access while the Underbright flickered through brownouts. The way my Examination pin gave me access to archives that Underbright residents—people just as smart, just as curious—would never see.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Coming home always reminded me why I'd left.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: "Miss Paradox, may I suggest returning to your quarters? Cross-referencing Mochi's map data will take several hours. You could use rest."

emphasis_words: Miss Paradox, may I suggest returning to your quarters? Cross-referencing Mochi's map data will take several hours. You could use rest.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: He wasn't wrong. My hands were shaking—adrenaline crash meeting three days of worry about Mochi. But sleep felt impossible when we'd just discovered forty-seven targets painted across Aethon, half of them already in someone else's crosshairs.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Show me the closest one,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Arc's holographic projection flickered through coordinates. "Filtering for safety, accessibility, and probability of intact Keeper... complete. The nearest viable location is in the Nexus itself. The Meridian Junction, subsection four. Eleven kilometers from here."

emphasis_words: Filtering for safety, accessibility, and probability of intact Keeper... complete. The nearest viable location is in the Nexus itself. The Meridian Junction, subsection four. Eleven kilometers from here.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Here?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Makes sense."

segments.speaker: PAGE_TURN
segments.text: [DING - page turn cue]

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The bitterness in his voice surprised me. Clive rarely cared about politics. But then, Clive was older than the Nexus itself. Maybe he remembered when this land was something else. Before the Council. Before the Examination. Before we decided some people deserved the light more than others.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi pulsed blue-green—detection mixed with agreement. The device drifted toward the archives exit, pointing.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Mochi wants to go,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Of course Mochi wants to go."

emphasis_words: Of course Mochi wants to go.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: "Miss Paradox, it is 3:47 AM. The Meridian Junction will still be present in daylight. I would strongly recommend—"

emphasis_words: Miss Paradox, it is 3:47 AM. The Meridian Junction will still be present in daylight. I would strongly recommend—

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "We're going now."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "And if they're waiting for us there?"

emphasis_words: And if they're waiting for us there?

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Then at least we'll know who we're dealing with."

emphasis_words: Then at least we'll know who we're dealing with.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Clive made a grinding sound that might've been a laugh. "That's the kind of logic that gets people killed."

emphasis_words: That's the kind of logic that gets people killed.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "You coming or not?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "I've outlived worse decisions."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The Meridian Junction was a transportation hub I'd passed through a thousand times. Six rail lines converging beneath an Art Nouveau dome of glass and silver, Signal-light fixtures casting everything in perpetual blue-white glow. The dome's curves were pure Nexus aesthetic—organic lines meeting geometric precision, every surface decorated with holographic announcements and arrival times that shifted like living poetry.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: At nearly four in the morning, it was almost empty—just a few night-shift workers heading to the industrial districts and one sleeping transit guard. The guard wore an Underbright pin on her collar. Not eligible to vote. Just eligible to work the hours nobody with Examination clearance wanted.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: We descended to subsection four: maintenance access, storage, forgotten rooms that held equipment for systems nobody remembered how to repair. The air smelled like ozone and old stone. Arc illuminated our path, holographic light mixing with Mochi's steady blue pulse.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The Nexus liked to think of itself as a meritocracy. Knowledge as the highest virtue. The Illuminated Path teaching that understanding was available to anyone willing to seek it. But down here, in the maintenance sublevels where the Signal-powered infrastructure actually ran, you could see the truth. Some people sought Clarity in libraries. Some people just kept the lights on.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Getting stronger,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Too close."

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: "Miss Paradox, I'm detecting unusual interference patterns ahead. Consistent with Signal-dampening technology."

emphasis_words: Miss Paradox, I'm detecting unusual interference patterns ahead. Consistent with Signal-dampening technology.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: My blood went cold. "The Quiet was here."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Is here,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi flashed red—danger. Then determinedly pushed forward anyway, into the interference field.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Mochi, wait—"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: But the device didn't stop. Couldn't stop, maybe. The Keeper memories were pulling, and Mochi had already spent three days trying to get us here. Turning back wasn't an option.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: So I followed. Of course I followed.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The dampening field hit like walking into water. Pressure on my ears, my teeth, my bones—the Signal's absence made physical. Arc's light flickered. Clive's symbols dimmed. Only Mochi's glow persisted, stubborn violet pushing through interference.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The corridor opened into a chamber I'd never known existed.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: It wasn't large—maybe twenty feet across, circular, carved from the bedrock the Nexus was built on. And in the center, half-buried in the floor: a Keeper.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Larger than Mochi. Crystalline and dark, geometric form covered in Pillar markings. Dormant. Silent. But intact. Untouched by The Quiet's usual destruction.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Because someone was already here.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: A figure in dark unmarked clothing crouched beside the Keeper, hands hovering over its surface. No weapons. No tools. Just... examining. Their face was obscured by a hood, but they turned when we entered. Stood slowly.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Independent explorer,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Not "Independent Chronicler." They knew I wasn't Chronicle. Knew I'd never joined in the first place—that I'd passed the Examination but chose a different path. Independent documentation instead of institutional preservation.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Who are you?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Someone who wants the same thing you do: understanding."

emphasis_words: Someone who wants the same thing you do: understanding.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "You're Quiet,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The figure's hood shifted—maybe a nod. "We prefer 'preservationists.' We preserve Aethon by ensuring certain knowledge stays buried."

emphasis_words: We prefer 'preservationists.' We preserve Aethon by ensuring certain knowledge stays buried.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "You destroy Keepers."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Those were necessary."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I'd heard that logic before. Different words, same meaning. The Illuminated Path taught that knowledge was sacred, that understanding was available to all—but somehow, the Council always decided who got access to which understanding. Who passed which Examination. Who could join institutions like the Chronicle and who stayed independent, documenting from the margins.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: "Miss Paradox,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi vibrated urgent warning against my chest, colors flashing orange-red-purple in rapid succession. Sensing something we couldn't.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "The Keepers hold answers about the Dimming,"

emphasis_words: The Keepers hold answers about the Dimming,

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Inconvenient."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: They stepped closer, and I could see their hands now—calloused, Signal-marked, the kind of scarring that came from working directly with Pillar technology. Not a scholar's hands. Not a Council member's hands.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Every Keeper awakening destabilizes Pillars,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I thought of that moment—the first Keeper waking, showing us visions of the Pillars being built. Clive's recognition. Mochi's fascinated purple. And somewhere across Aethon, Pillars stuttering in response.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: And I thought of the Council's response when I'd tried to report it. "File it with the Chronicle. We'll review it in the next session. Knowledge requires proper channels, Miss Paradox."

emphasis_words: File it with the Chronicle. We'll review it in the next session. Knowledge requires proper channels, Miss Paradox.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Three months later, still under review. While Pillars failed. While the Underbright dimmed.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Same argument you made in the warehouse,"

emphasis_words: Same argument you made in the warehouse,

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Or you'll wake something that should have stayed asleep and accelerate the end."

emphasis_words: Or you'll wake something that should have stayed asleep and accelerate the end.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "I'm sorry,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: But I couldn't let them destroy what might be our only chance at answers.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Clive,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The ancient automaton's core light flared brilliant blue, and the chamber filled with a resonance frequency that made my teeth ache and my bones vibrate. Not an attack—a Signal pulse, carefully calibrated to interact with the dampening field in exactly the wrong way.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The field collapsed with a sound like reality tearing. The Quiet agent stumbled, their dampening equipment sparking and dying. Arc's light blazed back to full strength. Mochi pulsed triumphant green.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Run!"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: We didn't wait to see what.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The passage was narrow, barely tall enough to run through hunched over. My shoulders scraped brick as I followed Clive's pink glow through turns I couldn't track. Left, right, down a rusted ladder into older infrastructure. The air changed—damper, colder, carrying the mineral smell of deep earth.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Service tunnels,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Behind us: footsteps. The agent was following. Fast.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: We burst through a rusted door into chaos—an Underbright market district just starting to wake. Street vendors setting up stalls. Workers heading to early shifts. People living in the city the Council's beautiful philosophy had built and then forgotten about.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Everyone okay?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi pulsed soft pink from where the device had landed on my shoulder. Clive was already upright, his glowing head sweeping the street for threats. Arc's presence hummed reassuringly on my wrist.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "The Keeper?"

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: "Still dormant,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "For now,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I looked around the Underbright market. At people who'd never see the inside of a Council chamber. Who'd never pass the Examination, not because they weren't smart enough, but because the test was designed to filter for a specific kind of knowledge that had nothing to do with intelligence and everything to do with access.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: At people who'd lose Signal first when Pillars failed. Who'd be told it was for the greater good. That Clarity required sacrifice.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Then we need to move it,"

emphasis_words: Then we need to move it,

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: "Miss Paradox, relocating a dormant Keeper would require specialized equipment, Council authorization, and—"

emphasis_words: Miss Paradox, relocating a dormant Keeper would require specialized equipment, Council authorization, and—

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "And nothing."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "So what do you propose?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I thought of the agent's calloused hands. Their bitterness about the Council. The way they'd known exactly how the Illuminated Path's beautiful ideals had calcified into gatekeeping.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I thought of Mochi, carrying memories of forty-seven Keepers. Some already dark. Some waiting to be found. Some sleeping beneath cities that had forgotten they were there.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: And I thought of home. The Nexus. The city that had taught me knowledge was sacred and then shown me all the ways that sacredness could be weaponized.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "We do what we always do,"

emphasis_words: We do what we always do,

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "That is not a detailed tactical plan,"

emphasis_words: That is not a detailed tactical plan,

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "It's the best one we've got."

emphasis_words: It's the best one we've got.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Clive made a sound that might have been approval. "Kid's finally learning. The tall ones didn't wait for approval either. They just built what needed building."

emphasis_words: Kid's finally learning. The tall ones didn't wait for approval either. They just built what needed building.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi pulsed warm agreement against my neck, and despite everything—the Quiet, the Council, the forty-seven Keepers slowly dying across Aethon—I felt something like hope.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: We'd found a map. We'd protected a Keeper. We'd survived another night.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Tomorrow, we'd figure out the rest.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: *chronicle entry:* Mochi has been carrying memories from the Keeper that died in Memorywreck Bay—memories of where other Keepers sleep across Aethon. Forty-seven locations. Some intact. Some already destroyed by The Quiet. Some we might still save.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: We found one tonight, beneath the Meridian Junction. The Quiet tried to destroy it. Clive stopped them. The Keeper survives. But the agent said something that's been sitting heavy in my chest: awakening Keepers destabilizes the Signal. Every time we touch these memory-guardians, Pillars flicker across the region.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: So what do we do? Let The Quiet erase the only knowledge that might save us? Or risk accelerating the Dimming by waking what was meant to sleep?

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I don't know. But I know Mochi spent three days processing inherited memories, trying to find a way to show us the truth. I know that device—no voice, no way to explain—worked harder than any of us to make us understand.

segments.speaker: PAGE_TURN
segments.text: [DING - page turn cue]

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I know the Council will spend months debating while Keepers die. I know the Illuminated Path promises enlightenment to everyone while keeping the Underbright dim. I know the Nexus taught me that knowledge is sacred and then showed me exactly how that sacredness gets used to decide who deserves light.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: And I know that coming home always reminds me why I left.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi's not a pet. Not a mascot. Mochi's a Keeper-kin, carrying purpose the rest of us can barely comprehend. And if Mochi thinks we need to find the sleeping guardians, then that's what we're going to do.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Seventeen Keepers are reachable. The Quiet has a head start. The Council has committees.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: We have each other, a map only we can read, and the stubborn belief that some truths matter more than the people trying to bury them.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Time to even the odds.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: We document. We preserve. We remember. Even when the memories fight back. Even when the people who should be helping are the ones standing in the way.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: -P.P.
